name: app.R
content:
# =============================================================================
# Red bipartita de compradores y contratistas — GuateCompras
# Bipartite network of public BUYERS and the CONTRACTORS that obtained awards.
#
# Runs in-browser via shinylive / WebAssembly.
#
# Bilingual UI (Español / English): only the chrome (title, description,
# labels) is translated — the graph rendering (nodes, edges, legend, tooltips)
# is unchanged between languages.
#
# TIPO DE RED (global toggle): all contracts vs. only single-bidder ("no
# competition") contracts.
#
# THREE views (buttons):
#   1. Compradores y Contratistas — whole network; a menu filters buyers by
#      TYPE or by SPECIFIC buyers; plus "both presidential periods" filter.
#   2. Periodo presidencial — one presidency; year slider clamped to it.
#   3. Contratistas — one or more specific contractors.
# All views render statically (stabilize once, then freeze; still draggable).
# A year-range slider and a "strongest links" slider apply in every view.
#
# Data: data/network.rds  (built by scripts/prep_network_data.R)
# =============================================================================

library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(visNetwork)
library(data.table)

`%||%` <- function(a, b) if (is.null(a) || length(a) == 0) b else a

NET <- readRDS("data/network.rds")
edges_all   <- as.data.table(NET$edges)
buyers      <- as.data.table(NET$buyers)
contractors <- as.data.table(NET$contractors)
YEARS  <- NET$years
YR_MIN <- min(YEARS); YR_MAX <- max(YEARS)
BUYER_TYPES <- NET$buyer_types            # data values (Spanish)

buyer_choices      <- setNames(buyers$buyer_id, buyers$buyer_name)
contractor_choices <- setNames(contractors$contractor_id, contractors$contractor_name)

fmt_q <- function(x) paste0("Q", formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ","))

# A control label carrying a hover explanation (native browser tooltip on the
# title text). Renders with a dotted underline + help cursor via .tip-label.
tip <- function(text, msg) tags$span(text, title = msg, class = "tip-label")

DL_URL <- "https://ocds.guatecompras.gt/descarga-datos"

# --- Buyer type -> visNetwork group (color) --------------------------------
TYPE_GROUP <- c(Ministerios = "Ministerio", Municipalidades = "Municipalidad",
                `Otras entidades` = "Otra entidad")
COL <- list(Ministerio = "#1f6feb", Municipalidad = "#8250df",
            `Otra entidad` = "#cf222e", Contratista = "#8b949e")

MODES <- c("main", "contractors")

# ============================== I18N =======================================
I18N <- list(
  es = list(
    title = "Red de compradores públicos y contratistas — GuateCompras",
    btn_main = "Compradores y Contratistas",
    btn_period = "Periodo presidencial",
    btn_contractors = "Contratistas",
    desc_main = paste("Compradores y Contratistas: explore toda la red. Use el",
      "menú para filtrar por tipo de comprador o elegir compradores",
      "específicos; las demás opciones controlan el tipo de red, los años y los",
      "contratistas activos en ambos periodos presidenciales. Al elegir",
      "compradores específicos, la red incluye también los otros compradores",
      "que comparten contratistas con su selección."),
    desc_period = paste("Periodo presidencial: muestra las adjudicaciones de una",
      "presidencia. Los años del deslizador se limitan al periodo elegido."),
    desc_contractors = paste("Contratistas: elija uno o varios contratistas para",
      "ver de qué compradores recibieron adjudicaciones."),
    net_type_label = "Tipo de red:",
    net_all = "Todos los contratos",
    net_single = "Solo contratos sin competencia (un oferente)",
    year_label = "Años:",
    topn_label = "Vínculos más fuertes a mostrar:",
    buyerfilter_label = "Filtrar compradores por:",
    bf_all = "Todos", bf_type = "Tipo de comprador", bf_specific = "Compradores específicos",
    type_label = "Tipo de comprador (uno):",
    type_names = c("Ministerios", "Municipalidades", "Otras entidades"),
    specific_label = "Compradores (uno o varios):",
    period_label = "Periodo presidencial (uno):",
    period_gia = "Presidencia de Giammattei", period_are = "Presidencia de Arevalo",
    contractors_label = "Contratistas (uno o varios):",
    both_periods = "Solo contratistas presentes en ambos periodos presidenciales",
    placeholder = "Escriba para buscar…",
    status_need = "Seleccione al menos una opción para mostrar la red.",
    status_show = "Mostrando %s de %s vínculos comprador→contratista (%s; los más fuertes).",
    net_lbl_all = "todos los contratos", net_lbl_single = "contratos sin competencia",
    no_data = "Sin datos para la selección actual.",
    tip_net_type = "Elija ver todas las adjudicaciones o solo las de contrataciones con un único oferente (contratos sin competencia).",
    tip_buyerfilter = "Limite la red a un tipo de comprador (Ministerios, Municipalidades u Otras) o a compradores específicos. Al elegir compradores específicos, se muestran también los otros compradores que comparten contratistas con su selección.",
    tip_type = "Muestra únicamente los compradores del tipo seleccionado.",
    tip_specific = "Escriba para buscar y seleccione uno o más compradores en los que enfocarse; quedarán al centro del grafo.",
    tip_both_periods = "Muestra solo los contratistas que recibieron adjudicaciones tanto durante la presidencia de Giammattei (2020–2024) como la de Arévalo (2024–2028).",
    tip_year = "Limite la red a las adjudicaciones cuyo año (según la fecha de la oferta ganadora) esté dentro del rango seleccionado.",
    tip_contractors = "Escriba para buscar y seleccione uno o más contratistas para ver de qué compradores recibieron adjudicaciones.",
    tip_topn = "Número máximo de vínculos a dibujar, ordenados de mayor a menor número de adjudicaciones. Súbalo para ver más relaciones (puede ser más lento)."
  ),
  en = list(
    title = "Network of public buyers and contractors — GuateCompras",
    btn_main = "Buyers & Contractors",
    btn_period = "Presidential period",
    btn_contractors = "Contractors",
    desc_main = paste("Buyers & Contractors: explore the whole network. Use the",
      "menu to filter by buyer type or pick specific buyers; the other options",
      "control network type, years, and contractors active in both presidential",
      "periods. When you pick specific buyers, the network also includes the",
      "other buyers that share contractors with your selection."),
    desc_period = paste("Presidential period: shows the awards of one presidency.",
      "The year slider is limited to the chosen period."),
    desc_contractors = paste("Contractors: pick one or more contractors to see",
      "which buyers awarded them contracts."),
    net_type_label = "Network type:",
    net_all = "All contracts",
    net_single = "Only single-bidder contracts (no competition)",
    year_label = "Years:",
    topn_label = "Strongest links to show:",
    buyerfilter_label = "Filter buyers by:",
    bf_all = "All", bf_type = "Buyer type", bf_specific = "Specific buyers",
    type_label = "Buyer type (one):",
    type_names = c("Ministries", "Municipalities", "Other entities"),
    specific_label = "Buyers (one or more):",
    period_label = "Presidential period (one):",
    period_gia = "Presidency of Giammattei", period_are = "Presidency of Arevalo",
    contractors_label = "Contractors (one or more):",
    both_periods = "Only contractors present in both presidential periods",
    placeholder = "Type to search…",
    status_need = "Select at least one option to display the network.",
    status_show = "Showing %s of %s buyer→contractor links (%s; strongest shown).",
    net_lbl_all = "all contracts", net_lbl_single = "single-bidder contracts",
    no_data = "No data for the current selection.",
    tip_net_type = "Choose whether to show all awards or only those from tenders with a single bidder (contracts awarded without competition).",
    tip_buyerfilter = "Restrict the network to a buyer type (Ministries, Municipalities or Other) or to specific buyers. When you pick specific buyers, the network also shows the other buyers that share contractors with your selection.",
    tip_type = "Shows only buyers of the selected type.",
    tip_specific = "Type to search and select one or more buyers to focus on; they are placed at the center of the graph.",
    tip_both_periods = "Shows only contractors that received awards during both the Giammattei presidency (2020–2024) and the Arévalo presidency (2024–2028).",
    tip_year = "Restrict the network to awards whose year (based on the winning bid date) falls within the selected range.",
    tip_contractors = "Type to search and select one or more contractors to see which buyers awarded them contracts.",
    tip_topn = "Maximum number of links to draw, ordered from most to fewest awards. Increase it to see more relationships (may be slower)."
  )
)

# Top description, per language. The lead sentence is always visible; the data
# source and legend paragraphs are tucked into a collapsible <details> so the
# header stays compact on small (laptop) screens.
desc_ui <- function(lang) {
  if (lang == "en") {
    tagList(
      tags$p(class = "app-sub",
        "This app visualizes Guatemala's public-procurement network as a bipartite ",
        "graph linking government buyers to the contractors (suppliers) that were ",
        "awarded contracts. Each link connects a buyer to a contractor it awarded ",
        "at least one contract to."),
      tags$details(class = "app-details",
        tags$summary("More about the data and how to read the graph"),
        tags$p(class = "app-sub",
          "The data come from ", tags$b("GuateCompras"), ", Guatemala's public ",
          "procurement system (Sistema de Información de Contrataciones y Adquisiciones ",
          "del Estado), published under the ", tags$b("Open Contracting Data Standard (OCDS)"),
          ". They cover awards recorded ", tags$b("between January 2020 and July 2026"),
          " (based on the winning bid date). To download the data directly, visit the ",
          "open data portal of GuateCompras and the Ministry of Public Finance: ",
          tags$a(href = DL_URL, DL_URL, target = "_blank"), "."),
        tags$p(class = "app-sub",
          tags$b("Edge thickness"), " = number of awards; ", tags$b("node size"),
          " = total awards the entity is involved in; ", tags$b("buyer color"),
          " indicates its type (Ministries, Municipalities, or Other entities). ",
          "Hover over a link or node to see the total awarded value (GTQ).")))
  } else {
    tagList(
      tags$p(class = "app-sub",
        "Esta aplicación visualiza la red de contrataciones públicas de Guatemala ",
        "como un grafo bipartito que conecta a las entidades compradoras del Estado ",
        "con los contratistas (proveedores) que obtuvieron adjudicaciones. Cada ",
        "vínculo une a un comprador con un contratista al que le adjudicó al menos ",
        "un contrato."),
      tags$details(class = "app-details",
        tags$summary("Más información sobre los datos y cómo leer el grafo"),
        tags$p(class = "app-sub",
          "Los datos provienen de ", tags$b("GuateCompras"), ", el Sistema de ",
          "Información de Contrataciones y Adquisiciones del Estado de Guatemala, ",
          "publicados bajo el ", tags$b("Estándar de Datos de Contrataciones Abiertas (OCDS)"),
          ". Incluyen las adjudicaciones registradas ",
          tags$b("entre enero de 2020 y julio de 2026"),
          " (según la fecha de la oferta ganadora). Para descargar directamente los ",
          "datos, ingrese al portal de datos abiertos de GuateCompras y el Ministerio ",
          "de Finanzas Públicas: ", tags$a(href = DL_URL, DL_URL, target = "_blank"), "."),
        tags$p(class = "app-sub",
          tags$b("Grosor del vínculo"), " = número de adjudicaciones; ",
          tags$b("tamaño del nodo"), " = total de adjudicaciones en que participa la ",
          "entidad; el ", tags$b("color del comprador"), " indica su tipo (Ministerios, ",
          "Municipalidades u Otras entidades). Pase el cursor sobre un vínculo o nodo ",
          "para ver el valor total adjudicado (GTQ).")))
  }
}

# ============================== UI =========================================
ui <- page_fluid(
  theme = bs_theme(version = 5, primary = "#1f6feb"),
  tags$head(tags$style(HTML("
    body { overflow-y: auto; }
    .mode-bar .btn { margin: 2px; }
    .control-panel { background:#f6f8fa; border:1px solid #d0d7de;
                     border-radius:8px; padding:8px 12px; margin-bottom:6px; }
    .app-title { font-weight:700; font-size:1.3rem; margin-bottom:2px; }
    .app-sub   { color:#57606a; font-size:0.86rem; margin:2px 0; }
    .app-details > summary { cursor:pointer; color:#1f6feb; font-size:0.86rem;
                             margin:2px 0 4px; }
    .view-desc { background:#eef4ff; border-left:3px solid #1f6feb;
                 padding:6px 10px; border-radius:4px; font-size:0.88rem;
                 margin:6px 0; color:#24324a; }
    .lang-box { text-align:right; }
    /* Menu titles with a hover explanation get a dotted underline + help cursor */
    .tip-label { border-bottom: 1px dotted #8a8a8a; cursor: help; }
    /* Graph fills the space that's left, but never collapses to a sliver. */
    #net { height: calc(100vh - 520px) !important; min-height: 460px !important; }
  ")),
  tags$script(HTML("
    Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler('setMode', function(m){
      document.querySelectorAll('.mode-btn').forEach(function(b){
        var on = (b.id === 'mode_' + m);
        b.classList.toggle('btn-primary', on);
        b.classList.toggle('btn-outline-primary', !on);
      });
    });
  "))),

  fluidRow(
    column(9, uiOutput("title_ui")),
    column(3, div(class = "lang-box",
      radioButtons("lang", NULL, c("Español" = "es", "English" = "en"),
                   selected = "es", inline = TRUE)))
  ),
  uiOutput("desc_ui"),

  div(class = "mode-bar",
      actionButton("mode_main",        I18N$es$btn_main,        class = "btn mode-btn btn-primary"),
      actionButton("mode_contractors", I18N$es$btn_contractors, class = "btn mode-btn btn-outline-primary")),
  uiOutput("view_desc"),

  div(class = "control-panel",
      fluidRow(
        column(5, uiOutput("mode_control")),
        column(4, uiOutput("net_type_ui"), uiOutput("year_ui")),
        column(3, uiOutput("topn_ui"))
      )),

  textOutput("status"),
  visNetworkOutput("net", height = "600px")
)

# ============================== SERVER =====================================
server <- function(input, output, session) {

  tr   <- reactive(I18N[[input$lang %||% "es"]])
  mode <- reactiveVal("main")

  lapply(MODES, function(m)
    observeEvent(input[[paste0("mode_", m)]], mode(m), ignoreInit = TRUE))
  observe(session$sendCustomMessage("setMode", mode()))

  output$title_ui <- renderUI(div(class = "app-title", tr()$title))
  output$desc_ui  <- renderUI(desc_ui(input$lang %||% "es"))
  output$view_desc <- renderUI({
    t <- tr()
    div(class = "view-desc",
        switch(mode(), main = t$desc_main, contractors = t$desc_contractors))
  })

  # Re-label the mode buttons when the language changes (labels only; the other
  # controls are rendered per-language below and translate themselves).
  observeEvent(input$lang, {
    t <- tr()
    updateActionButton(session, "mode_main",        label = t$btn_main)
    updateActionButton(session, "mode_contractors", label = t$btn_contractors)
  })

  # Global controls, rendered per language with a hover tooltip on each title.
  # Values are preserved across language switches via isolate().
  output$net_type_ui <- renderUI({
    t <- tr()
    radioButtons("net_type", tip(t$net_type_label, t$tip_net_type),
      choiceNames = list(t$net_all, t$net_single),
      choiceValues = list("all", "single"),
      selected = isolate(input$net_type) %||% "all")
  })
  output$year_ui <- renderUI({
    t <- tr()
    sliderInput("year_range", tip(t$year_label, t$tip_year),
      min = YR_MIN, max = YR_MAX,
      value = isolate(input$year_range) %||% c(YR_MIN, YR_MAX),
      step = 1, sep = "", width = "100%")
  })
  output$topn_ui <- renderUI({
    t <- tr()
    sliderInput("top_n", tip(t$topn_label, t$tip_topn),
      min = 50, max = 1500, value = isolate(input$top_n) %||% 300,
      step = 50, width = "100%")
  })

  # View-specific control ---------------------------------------------------
  output$mode_control <- renderUI({
    t <- tr()
    switch(mode(),
      main = tagList(
        selectInput("buyer_filter", tip(t$buyerfilter_label, t$tip_buyerfilter),
          choices = setNames(c("all", "type", "specific"),
                             c(t$bf_all, t$bf_type, t$bf_specific)),
          selected = isolate(input$buyer_filter) %||% "all", width = "100%"),
        uiOutput("buyer_sub"),
        checkboxInput("both_periods", tip(t$both_periods, t$tip_both_periods),
                      value = isolate(input$both_periods) %||% FALSE)),
      contractors = selectizeInput("sel_contractors",
        tip(t$contractors_label, t$tip_contractors),
        choices = contractor_choices, multiple = TRUE, width = "100%",
        options = list(placeholder = t$placeholder, maxOptions = 1000))
    )
  })

  # Sub-control for the buyer filter menu (type radio OR specific selectize)
  output$buyer_sub <- renderUI({
    t <- tr()
    req(mode() == "main")
    switch(input$buyer_filter %||% "all",
      type = radioButtons("sel_type", tip(t$type_label, t$tip_type),
        choices = setNames(BUYER_TYPES, t$type_names), selected = BUYER_TYPES[1]),
      specific = selectizeInput("sel_buyers", tip(t$specific_label, t$tip_specific),
        choices = buyer_choices, multiple = TRUE, width = "100%",
        options = list(placeholder = t$placeholder, maxOptions = 1000)),
      NULL)
  })

  # --- Filtering pipeline --------------------------------------------------
  mode_filtered <- reactive({
    e <- edges_all
    if (identical(input$net_type, "single")) e <- e[single_bidder == TRUE]
    yr <- input$year_range
    if (!is.null(yr)) e <- e[purchase_year >= yr[1] & purchase_year <= yr[2]]

    m <- mode()
    if (m == "main") {
      bf <- input$buyer_filter %||% "all"
      if (bf == "type" && !is.null(input$sel_type)) {
        e <- e[buyer_type == input$sel_type]
      } else if (bf == "specific") {
        if (length(input$sel_buyers)) {
          # Keep the selected buyers' contractors, and every buyer that also
          # contracts with those contractors -- but NOT those other buyers'
          # remaining contractors. i.e. keep every edge that touches one of the
          # selected buyers' contractors, and nothing beyond.
          c1 <- unique(e[buyer_id %in% input$sel_buyers]$contractor_id)
          e  <- e[contractor_id %in% c1]
        } else {
          e <- e[0]
        }
      }
      if (isTRUE(input$both_periods) && nrow(e)) {
        both <- e[!is.na(presidential_period),
                  .(np = uniqueN(presidential_period)), by = contractor_id][np == 2L, contractor_id]
        e <- e[contractor_id %in% both]
      }
    } else if (m == "contractors") {
      e <- if (length(input$sel_contractors)) e[contractor_id %in% input$sel_contractors] else e[0]
    }
    e
  })

  agg_edges <- reactive({
    e <- mode_filtered()
    if (!nrow(e)) return(e[, .(buyer_id, contractor_id, n_awards, value_gtq)][0])
    e[, .(n_awards = sum(n_awards), value_gtq = sum(value_gtq)),
      by = .(buyer_id, contractor_id)]
  })

  top_edges <- reactive({
    e <- agg_edges()
    if (!nrow(e)) return(e)
    setorder(e, -n_awards, -value_gtq)
    n <- input$top_n
    # When focusing on specific buyers, always keep their own edges (so the
    # selected buyer is present and can be centered), then fill up to n with the
    # strongest remaining edges.
    if (identical(mode(), "main") && identical(input$buyer_filter, "specific") &&
        length(input$sel_buyers)) {
      sel  <- e[buyer_id %in% input$sel_buyers]
      if (nrow(sel) >= n) return(head(sel, n))
      rest <- head(e[!buyer_id %in% input$sel_buyers], n - nrow(sel))
      return(rbind(sel, rest))
    }
    head(e, n)
  })

  # Selected buyers to pin at the center of the graph (specific-buyer focus only)
  center_ids <- reactive({
    if (identical(mode(), "main") && identical(input$buyer_filter, "specific"))
      input$sel_buyers %||% character(0) else character(0)
  })

  output$status <- renderText({
    t <- tr(); e <- agg_edges()
    needs <- (mode() == "contractors" && !length(input$sel_contractors)) ||
             (mode() == "main" && identical(input$buyer_filter, "specific") &&
                !length(input$sel_buyers))
    if (needs) return(t$status_need)
    net_lbl <- if (identical(input$net_type, "single")) t$net_lbl_single else t$net_lbl_all
    sprintf(t$status_show, formatC(nrow(top_edges()), big.mark = ","),
            formatC(nrow(e), big.mark = ","), net_lbl)
  })

  # --- Render network (static; identical across languages) -----------------
  output$net <- renderVisNetwork({
    t <- tr(); e <- top_edges()
    validate(need(nrow(e) > 0, t$no_data))

    b_ids <- unique(e$buyer_id); c_ids <- unique(e$contractor_id)
    b_str <- e[, .(s = sum(n_awards), v = sum(value_gtq)), by = buyer_id]
    c_str <- e[, .(s = sum(n_awards), v = sum(value_gtq)), by = contractor_id]
    bn <- buyers[buyer_id %in% b_ids]; cn <- contractors[contractor_id %in% c_ids]

    scale_size <- function(s) 10 + 25 * (s - min(s)) / (max(s) - min(s) + 1e-9)
    show_labels <- (length(b_ids) + length(c_ids)) <= 60

    bm <- b_str[match(bn$buyer_id, buyer_id)]
    cm <- c_str[match(cn$contractor_id, contractor_id)]

    # Pin selected buyers at the center so the layout forms around them, and
    # emphasize them (thick dark border + shadow) so the center is identifiable.
    is_center <- bn$buyer_id %in% center_ids()
    bx <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(bn)); by <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(bn))
    k <- sum(is_center)
    if (k == 1) { bx[is_center] <- 0; by[is_center] <- 0 }
    else if (k > 1) {                       # cluster several around the origin
      ang <- head(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = k + 1), k)
      bx[is_center] <- 70 * cos(ang); by[is_center] <- 70 * sin(ang)
    }
    b_bg <- unlist(COL[TYPE_GROUP[bn$buyer_type]], use.names = FALSE)

    nodes <- rbind(
      data.table(
        id = paste0("B_", bn$buyer_id),
        label = if (show_labels) bn$buyer_name else "",
        group = TYPE_GROUP[bn$buyer_type],
        title = paste0("<b>", bn$buyer_name, "</b><br>", bn$buyer_type,
                       "<br>Adjudicaciones: ", bm$s,
                       "<br>Valor total: ", fmt_q(bm$v)),
        value = scale_size(bm$s) * fifelse(is_center, 1.8, 1),
        x = bx, y = by, fixed = is_center,
        borderWidth = fifelse(is_center, 6, 1),
        shadow = is_center,
        color.background = fifelse(is_center, b_bg, NA_character_),
        color.border     = fifelse(is_center, "#111111", NA_character_)),
      data.table(
        id = paste0("C_", cn$contractor_id),
        label = if (show_labels) cn$contractor_name else "",
        group = "Contratista",
        title = paste0("<b>", cn$contractor_name, "</b><br>Contratista",
                       "<br>Tipo jurídico: ", cn$legal_type,
                       "<br>Adjudicaciones: ", cm$s,
                       "<br>Valor total: ", fmt_q(cm$v)),
        value = scale_size(cm$s),
        x = NA_real_, y = NA_real_, fixed = FALSE,
        borderWidth = 1, shadow = FALSE,
        color.background = NA_character_, color.border = NA_character_)
    )
    vedges <- data.table(
      from = paste0("B_", e$buyer_id), to = paste0("C_", e$contractor_id),
      value = e$n_awards,
      title = paste0("Adjudicaciones: ", e$n_awards,
                     "<br>Valor total: ", fmt_q(e$value_gtq)))

    vn <- visNetwork(nodes, vedges) |>
      visGroups(groupname = "Ministerio",    color = list(background = COL$Ministerio,    border = "#0b4bbd"), shape = "square") |>
      visGroups(groupname = "Municipalidad", color = list(background = COL$Municipalidad, border = "#5a2ca0"), shape = "square") |>
      visGroups(groupname = "Otra entidad",  color = list(background = COL$`Otra entidad`, border = "#a01722"), shape = "square") |>
      visGroups(groupname = "Contratista",   color = list(background = COL$Contratista,   border = "#57606a"), shape = "dot") |>
      visEdges(color = list(color = "#c9d1d9", highlight = "#1f6feb"), smooth = FALSE) |>
      visNodes(scaling = list(min = 10, max = 40)) |>
      visLayout(improvedLayout = FALSE) |>
      visLegend(width = 0.16, position = "right", main = "Tipo de entidad") |>
      visPhysics(solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
                 forceAtlas2Based = list(gravitationalConstant = -50),
                 stabilization = list(iterations = 150)) |>
      visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE)) |>
      visInteraction(hover = TRUE, tooltipDelay = 120, navigationButtons = TRUE)

    # Static in every view: stabilize once, then stop moving. When specific
    # buyers are pinned at the origin, also recenter the viewport on them (a
    # plain fit would center on the whole graph's bounding box instead).
    freeze <- if (length(center_ids()) > 0)
      paste0("function(){ this.setOptions({physics:false});",
             " this.fit({animation:false});",
             " this.moveTo({position:{x:0,y:0}, scale:this.getScale(),",
             " animation:false}); }")
    else
      "function(){ this.setOptions({physics:false}); }"
    vn |> visEvents(stabilizationIterationsDone = freeze)
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

name: README.md
content:
# Red de compradores públicos y contratistas — Shiny (shinylive) app

Interactive bipartite network of GuateCompras **buyers** and the **contractors**
that actually obtained awards from them. Runs entirely in the visitor's browser
via **shinylive** (WebAssembly) — no R server required — so it can be dropped
into a static site (e.g. a Jekyll/AcademicPages page on diego-romero.com).

## Files
```
shiny/procurement-network/
├── app.R                 # the Shiny app
├── data/network.rds      # pre-aggregated edge list (~1.5 MB) — the only data shipped
└── README.md             # this file
scripts/prep_network_data.R   # builds data/network.rds from data/processed/tender_bidder.csv
shiny/_site/                   # EXPORTED static site (78 MB) — this is what you host
```

## Features

**Bilingual** — a Español / English toggle (top-right) translates the title,
description, and all control labels. The graph rendering (nodes, edges, legend,
tooltips) is intentionally identical across languages.

**Tipo de red** (global toggle, applies in every view):
- *Todos los contratos* — every awarded buyer→contractor relationship
- *Solo contratos sin competencia* — only awards from tenders that received a bid
  from a **single firm** (no competition). Computed in the data prep as the
  `single_bidder` flag (# of distinct bidders per tender == 1).

**Three mutually-exclusive views** (buttons above the graph):

1. **Compradores y Contratistas** — the whole network. A *"Filtrar compradores
   por"* menu narrows buyers by **type** (*Ministerios* — name contains
   "Ministerio", **except** "Ministerio Público" — *Municipalidades*, *Otras
   entidades*) or by **specific buyers**. Also offers a checkbox to keep only
   contractors active in **both** presidential periods.
2. **Periodo presidencial** — *Presidencia de Giammattei* (2020-01-15…2024-01-14)
   or *Presidencia de Arevalo* (2024-01-15…2028-01-14). The year slider is
   clamped to the chosen presidency.
3. **Contratistas** — pick one or more specific contractors.

**Always available:** a **year-range slider** (applies within every view) and a
slider for how many of the strongest links to draw (default 300).

**Encodings:** edge thickness = number of awards; **node size = total awards the
entity is involved in**; hover shows total value (GTQ). Buyer color = type
(**Ministerios blue, Municipalidades purple, Otras red**); contractors gray.
All views render statically (stabilize once, then freeze; nodes stay draggable).

> Time filters (years, presidential period) use the **winning bid date**, i.e.
> when the purchase actually happened — not the OCDS file/compile year.

## Rebuilding

**1. Regenerate the data** (after refreshing `data/processed/tender_bidder.csv`):
```bash
Rscript scripts/prep_network_data.R
```

**2. Re-export the static site:**
```bash
Rscript -e 'shinylive::export("shiny/procurement-network", "shiny/_site")'
```
(Requires the `shinylive` and `visNetwork` R packages.)

**3. Preview locally** — shinylive needs to be served over HTTP (not opened as a
`file://`). Any static server works:
```bash
cd shiny/_site && python3 -m http.server 8000
# then open http://localhost:8000
```
First load takes ~30–90 s while the browser downloads the R runtime (cached
afterwards).

## Deploying to your Jekyll site (diego-romero.com)

The exported `shiny/_site/` folder is **plain static files** — host it like any
other page.

1. Copy the exported site into your website repo under a subfolder, e.g.:
   ```
   <your-site-repo>/procurement-network/      <-- contents of shiny/_site/
   ```
2. Add a nav entry. In AcademicPages, edit `_data/navigation.yml`:
   ```yaml
   - title: "Procurement Network"
     url: /procurement-network/
   ```
   (Because `index.html` sits in that folder, the URL resolves automatically.)
3. Commit and push. GitHub Pages serves it as a new tab.

### Optional: embed inside a styled page instead of a bare tab
Create `_pages/procurement-network.md` with an iframe pointing at the app folder:
```html
---
title: "Procurement Network"
permalink: /procurement/
layout: single
---
<iframe src="/procurement-network/" style="width:100%;height:800px;border:0;"></iframe>
```

### Notes / caveats
- **No server, no COOP/COEP needed.** shinylive uses a service worker, which
  works on GitHub Pages out of the box.
- The site is ~78 MB (the R WebAssembly runtime + packages). That's fine for
  GitHub Pages (1 GB repo soft limit) but is large for `git` — consider keeping
  the export out of the main history or using a separate `gh-pages` branch.
- Only `data/network.rds` (~1.5 MB) ships as data — never the 2.9 GB
  `tender_bidder.csv`.
